text:
There was a little brown mouse named Benny. Every day, Benny explored the forest. He was particularly fond of dandelions. One sunny afternoon, Benny stumbled upon a majestic golden eagle perched atop a willow branch.

"Benny!" the eagle cried out. "Come here! I want to tell you something important!"

The small mouse paused and squinted his eyes. "What is it?" he asked cautiously.

The eagle closed its eyes again and tilted its head. "Life is ephemeral. One day, you'll soar high atop mountain peaks or glide gracefully over oceans. You'll catch large birds on your wings. And you'll mate and share eternity with kind-hearted mates. But..."

The eagle paused, breathing deeply. "...death has a way of sweeping everything away. Birds fall from the sky. Beavers dam up rivers. Trees fall silent. So embrace each moment."

Benny thought about this wisdom. "You're right. I didn't think about it that way."
